title: <div>Sources: The information and concepts in this codex are supported by a wide
content: Sources: The information and concepts in this codex are supported by a wide array of sources and examples across disciplines. For instance, Newton’s laws of motion and gravitation are documented in physics literature【7†L21-L28】【9†L13-L21】, Mendel’s and Darwin’s principles in biology texts【17†L1-L4】【20†L37-L44】, logical laws in philosophy references【37†L212-L219】, Occam’s Razor in philosophical and scientific discussions【44†L329-L335】, Hermetic and spiritual laws in esoteric writings【0†L1-L3】【0†L5-L8】, and narrative principles in literary theory (Chekhov’s advice)【63†L88-L96】 and screenwriting guides. The cross-domain patterns noted are synthesized from these diverse sources and the observed commonalities among them. Each entry’s citations highlight key formulations or evidence of these laws in their native context, demonstrating the rich tapestry of human understanding that spans from empirical science to abstract philosophy and creative art.
context: From document: 🧵 Meta-Laws of Reality — chunk 79 (chunk 79/79)
